question:
BTC ETFs first week combined AUM over $60 billion?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the combined AUM of all bitcoin spot ETFs as of end of day Jan 19, 2024 ET is over $60 billion. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No."

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible sources. This market may stay open until figures are known.

evidences.title: Statement on the Approval of Spot Bitcoin Exchange- ...
evidences.content: ### [About](https://www.sec.gov/about). * [Careers](https://www.sec.gov/about/careers-securities-exchange-commission). The U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia held that the Commission failed to adequately explain its reasoning in disapproving the listing and trading of GrayscaleέΑβs proposed ETP (the Grayscale Order).[[1]](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/speeches-statements/gensler-statement-spot-bitcoin-011023#_ftn1) The court therefore vacated the Grayscale Order and remanded the matter to the Commission. As IέΑβve said in the past, and without prejudging any one crypto asset, the vast majority of crypto assets are investment contracts and thus subject to the federal securities laws.[[2]](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/speeches-statements/gensler-statement-spot-bitcoin-011023#_ftn2). Furthermore, the Commission will fully investigate any fraud or manipulation in the securities markets, including schemes that use social media platforms.[[3]](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/speeches-statements/gensler-statement-spot-bitcoin-011023#_ftn3) Such regulated exchanges also have rules designed to address certain conflicts of interest as well as to protect investors and the public in...

evidences.title: January 2024 Month in Review: U.S. Spot Bitcoin ETFs ...
evidences.content: Ten years after the first spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund application was filed in the U.S., the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) finally approved spot Bitcoin ETFs on January 10, 2024. While the European markets have been offering crypto exchange-traded products (ETPs) since 2019, this is the first time U.S. investors can access the spot price of Bitcoin in a brokerage account via a familiar ETF structure. We believe this approval will help to expand the investor base for bitcoin in the U.S. and encourage regulators in other countries to reconsider their position in digital assets. The most obvious target market for spot bitcoin ETFs is the U.S. wealth management client assets, which would be approximately $44 trillion in size.1 Many of these investors were not able or willing to set up procedures to hold bitcoin directly.

evidences.title: prospectus - SEC.gov
evidences.content: Bitcoin ETF (f/k/a Hashdex Bitcoin Futures ETF, the έΑεFundέΑζ or έΑεDEFIέΑζ) is designed to provide investors. with price exposure to the bitcoin market. The Fund issues shares (έΑεSharesέΑζ) that trade on NYSE Arca stock exchange. Under its current investment objective, the Fund may hold bitcoin and bitcoin futures contracts. is subject to the risks of bitcoin as well as the additional risks of investing in the Fund. FundέΑβs investment objective is for changes in the SharesέΑβ net asset value (έΑεNAVέΑζ) to reflect the daily. The Benchmark is designed to track the price performance of bitcoin. Because the FundέΑβs investment objective is to track the price of the Benchmark, changes in. the price of the Shares may vary from changes in the spot price of bitcoin. investment in the Fund is subject to the risks of an investment in bitcoin and in futures contracts, both of which subject to. or sale resulting from the price volatility of bitcoin or Bitcoin Futures Contracts.

evidences.title: Bitwise Bitcoin ETF
evidences.content: The Trust will pay to the Sponsor a unitary management fee of 0.20% per annum of the TrustέΑβs bitcoin holdings (the έΑεSponsor FeeέΑζ). For an order to redeem a Basket, the Sponsor shall arrange for the Basket Amount of bitcoin to be sold and the cash proceeds (minus a per order transaction fee) distributed. Bitwise Investment Manager, LLC, an affiliate of the Sponsor, is expected to purchase the initial Baskets of Shares for $2,500,000, at a per-Share price of $25 for these 100,000 Shares (the έΑεSeed BasketsέΑζ). Such proceeds are expected to be used by the Trust to purchase bitcoin at or prior to the listing of Shares on the Exchange. **AN INVESTMENT IN THE TRUST MAY NOT BE SUITABLE FOR INVESTORS THAT ARE NOT IN A POSITION TO ACCEPT MORE RISK THAN MAY BE INVOLVED WITH OTHER EXCHANGE-TRADED PRODUCTS THAT DO NOT HOLD BITCOIN OR INTERESTS RELATED TO BITCOIN.

evidences.content: ### Important Risks Related to this Article Crypto assets, such as bitcoin and ether, are complex, generally exhibit extreme price volatility and unpredictability, and should be viewed as highly speculative assets. Crypto assets are frequently referred to as crypto έΑεcurrencies,έΑζ but they typically operate without central authority or banks, are not backed by any government or issuing entity (i.e., no right of recourse), have no government or insurance protections, are not legal tender and have limited or no usability as compared to fiat currencies. Federal, state or foreign governments may restrict the use, transfer, exchange and value of crypto assets, and regulation in the U.S. and worldwide is still developing. Excludes directly held securities and institutional assets. 2 Bitcoin mining = a way new bitcoins are entering circulation; a process via which bitcoin transactions are verified on the bitcoin blockchain.
